fever.description: Elevated body temperature, usually above 38°C (100.4°F). A sign the body is fighting infection.
fever.when_to_see_doctor: If fever exceeds 103°F (39.4°C), lasts more than 3 days, or is accompanied by rash, stiff neck, or difficulty breathing.

generic_medicines.name: Paracetamol (Acetaminophen)
generic_medicines.note: First choice for fever. Safe for adults and children.

generic_medicines.name: Ibuprofen
generic_medicines.note: Reduces fever and inflammation. Take with food.

generic_medicines.name: Aspirin
generic_medicines.note: Adults only. Do NOT give to children under 16.

headache.description: Pain or discomfort in the head or neck region. Can be tension-type, migraine, or secondary to another condition.
headache.when_to_see_doctor: Sudden severe headache, headache with fever and stiff neck, or headaches worsening over time.
home_remedies: rest in a dark quiet room

generic_medicines.name: Paracetamol (Acetaminophen)
generic_medicines.note: Best first choice for tension headache.

generic_medicines.name: Ibuprofen
generic_medicines.note: Good for headache with inflammation. Take with food.

generic_medicines.name: Aspirin
generic_medicines.note: Adults only. Effective for mild to moderate headache.

cough.description: Reflex action to clear the airways of mucus, irritants, or foreign particles.
cough.when_to_see_doctor: Cough lasting more than 3 weeks, coughing blood, or cough with high fever and shortness of breath.

generic_medicines.name: Dextromethorphan
generic_medicines.note: Dry cough suppressant. Do not use for productive cough.

generic_medicines.name: Guaifenesin
generic_medicines.note: Expectorant — loosens mucus. Drink plenty of water.

generic_medicines.name: Ambroxol
generic_medicines.note: Helps clear mucus from chest. Good for wet cough.

generic_medicines.name: Levosalbutamol + Ambroxol
generic_medicines.note: Combination syrup for cough with chest tightness.

sore throat.description: Pain, scratchiness, or irritation of the throat that often worsens when swallowing.
sore throat.when_to_see_doctor: Severe throat pain, difficulty swallowing or breathing, high fever, or symptoms lasting more than a week.

generic_medicines.name: Paracetamol
generic_medicines.note: Relieves throat pain and associated fever.

generic_medicines.name: Ibuprofen
generic_medicines.note: Reduces inflammation and pain in throat.

generic_medicines.name: Benzocaine lozenges
generic_medicines.note: Local anesthetic — numbs throat pain temporarily.

generic_medicines.name: Chlorhexidine gargle
generic_medicines.note: Antibacterial gargle for throat infection.

stomach pain.description: Pain or discomfort in the abdominal area. Causes vary widely from mild gas to serious conditions.
stomach pain.when_to_see_doctor: Severe sudden pain, pain with fever and vomiting, blood in stool, or pain lasting more than a few hours.

generic_medicines.name: Antacid (Aluminum + Magnesium hydroxide)
generic_medicines.note: For acidity and indigestion related stomach pain.

generic_medicines.name: Domperidone
generic_medicines.note: Reduces nausea and stomach cramping.

generic_medicines.name: Dicyclomine
generic_medicines.note: Antispasmodic — relieves stomach cramps and spasms.

generic_medicines.name: Pantoprazole
generic_medicines.note: For gastritis and acid-related stomach pain.

nausea.description: Sensation of unease in the stomach with an urge to vomit.
nausea.when_to_see_doctor: If nausea is persistent, accompanied by severe vomiting, chest pain, or signs of dehydration.
home_remedies: bland foods like rice and toast

generic_medicines.name: Domperidone
generic_medicines.note: Most common anti-nausea medicine in India.

generic_medicines.name: Ondansetron
generic_medicines.note: Stronger anti-nausea — used for severe nausea.

generic_medicines.name: Metoclopramide
generic_medicines.note: Helps stomach empty faster — reduces nausea.

generic_medicines.name: Dimenhydrinate
generic_medicines.note: For motion sickness related nausea.

diarrhea.description: Loose, watery stools occurring more than 3 times a day.
diarrhea.when_to_see_doctor: Lasts more than 2 days, signs of dehydration, blood in stool, or high fever.
home_remedies: BRAT diet banana rice applesauce toast

generic_medicines.name: ORS (Oral Rehydration Salts)
generic_medicines.note: MOST IMPORTANT — prevents dehydration. Start immediately.

generic_medicines.name: Loperamide
generic_medicines.note: Slows down bowel movement. Do not use if fever or blood in stool.

generic_medicines.name: Zinc Sulfate
generic_medicines.note: Recommended with ORS especially for children — shortens duration.

generic_medicines.name: Metronidazole
generic_medicines.note: If bacterial infection suspected — requires doctor advice.

fatigue.description: Extreme tiredness or exhaustion that does not improve with rest.
fatigue.when_to_see_doctor: Fatigue lasting more than 2 weeks, with unexplained weight loss, or affecting daily activities.

generic_medicines.name: Iron + Folic Acid
generic_medicines.note: If fatigue due to anemia. Take with Vitamin C for better absorption.

generic_medicines.name: Vitamin B12
generic_medicines.note: B12 deficiency is common cause of fatigue in India.

generic_medicines.name: Vitamin D3
generic_medicines.note: Deficiency causes fatigue and weakness — very common.

generic_medicines.name: Multivitamin
generic_medicines.note: General nutritional support for fatigue.

cold.description: Common viral infection of the upper respiratory tract causing runny nose, sneezing, and congestion.
cold.when_to_see_doctor: Symptoms lasting more than 10 days, high fever, or severe sinus pain.

generic_medicines.name: Cetirizine
generic_medicines.note: Antihistamine — reduces runny nose and sneezing.

generic_medicines.name: Phenylephrine (Decongestant)
generic_medicines.note: Relieves blocked nose.

generic_medicines.name: Paracetamol + Phenylephrine + Cetirizine
generic_medicines.note: Combination tablet for multiple cold symptoms.

generic_medicines.name: Saline nasal drops
generic_medicines.note: Safe for all ages — clears nasal passage naturally.

rash.description: Change in skin color or texture, often appearing as red patches, bumps, or blisters.
rash.when_to_see_doctor: Rash with fever, difficulty breathing, spreading rapidly, or signs of infection.

generic_medicines.name: Cetirizine
generic_medicines.note: Antihistamine — reduces itching and allergic rash.

generic_medicines.name: Calamine lotion
generic_medicines.note: Apply topically — soothes itching and irritation.

generic_medicines.name: Hydrocortisone cream (mild steroid)
generic_medicines.note: For inflammatory rashes. Short-term use only.

generic_medicines.name: Chlorpheniramine
generic_medicines.note: Antihistamine for allergic rash — causes drowsiness.

dizziness.description: Feeling of lightheadedness, unsteadiness, or spinning sensation.
dizziness.when_to_see_doctor: Dizziness with chest pain, sudden severe headache, fainting, or vision changes.

generic_medicines.name: Meclizine
generic_medicines.note: For vertigo and motion sickness related dizziness.

generic_medicines.name: Betahistine
generic_medicines.note: Specifically for vertigo and inner ear problems.

generic_medicines.name: ORS + Water
generic_medicines.note: If dizziness is due to dehydration — rehydrate immediately.

generic_medicines.name: Cinnarizine
generic_medicines.note: Reduces vertigo and motion sickness symptoms.

weakness.description: Lack of physical or muscular strength, feeling of tiredness in the body.
weakness.when_to_see_doctor: Sudden severe weakness, weakness on one side of body, or weakness with chest pain.

generic_medicines.name: Iron + Folic Acid
generic_medicines.note: For weakness due to anemia.

generic_medicines.name: Vitamin B Complex
generic_medicines.note: Boosts energy and reduces weakness.

generic_medicines.name: Vitamin D3
generic_medicines.note: Deficiency is major cause of weakness in India.

generic_medicines.name: ORS
generic_medicines.note: If weakness due to dehydration or illness.

chest pain.description: Pain or discomfort in the chest area. Can range from mild to severe and may indicate serious conditions.
chest pain.when_to_see_doctor: Any chest pain should be evaluated by a doctor immediately, especially with shortness of breath or sweating.
home_remedies: rest — if due to reflux try antacids, otherwise seek medical attention immediately

generic_medicines.name: Antacid (for acid reflux chest pain)
generic_medicines.note: ONLY if chest pain is clearly due to acidity. Seek doctor for any other type.

generic_medicines.name: Pantoprazole
generic_medicines.note: For GERD related chest discomfort — not for cardiac pain.

generic_medicines.name: WARNING
generic_medicines.note: Chest pain can be a medical emergency. Please consult a doctor immediately if unsure.

back pain.description: Pain in the lower, middle, or upper back. Very common and often related to muscle strain.
back pain.when_to_see_doctor: Back pain with numbness, weakness in legs, loss of bladder control, or after injury.

generic_medicines.name: Ibuprofen
generic_medicines.note: NSAID — reduces inflammation and back pain. Take with food.

generic_medicines.name: Diclofenac
generic_medicines.note: Apply gel topically or tablet form for back pain.

generic_medicines.name: Muscle Relaxant — Thiocolchicoside
generic_medicines.note: For muscle spasm related back pain — causes drowsiness.

generic_medicines.name: Paracetamol
generic_medicines.note: Milder option — first choice for simple back pain.

vomiting.description: Forceful expulsion of stomach contents through the mouth.
vomiting.when_to_see_doctor: Vomiting blood, vomiting lasting more than 24 hours, or with severe abdominal pain.
home_remedies: sip small amounts of water or ORS
avoid solid food for a few hours

generic_medicines.name: ORS (Oral Rehydration Salts)
generic_medicines.note: MOST IMPORTANT — prevents dehydration from vomiting.

generic_medicines.name: Ondansetron
generic_medicines.note: Strong anti-vomiting medicine. Works within 30 minutes.

generic_medicines.name: Domperidone
generic_medicines.note: Mild anti-vomiting — good for mild to moderate vomiting.

generic_medicines.name: Metoclopramide
generic_medicines.note: Helps settle stomach — reduces vomiting reflex.

paracetamol.dosage: Adults: 500mg to 1000mg every 4 to 6 hours. Max 4g per day. Children: weight-based, consult doctor.
paracetamol.warnings: Do not exceed recommended dose. Avoid alcohol. Caution in liver disease.

ibuprofen.dosage: Adults: 200mg to 400mg every 4 to 6 hours. Max 1200mg per day OTC. Take with food.
ibuprofen.warnings: Avoid on empty stomach. Caution in kidney disease, heart disease, and pregnancy. Not for children under 6 months.

amoxicillin.dosage: As prescribed by doctor. Typically 250mg to 500mg every 8 hours. Complete the full course.
amoxicillin.warnings: Antibiotic requires prescription. Do not stop early. Inform doctor of penicillin allergy.

cetirizine.dosage: Adults: 10mg once daily. Children 6 to 12: 5mg twice daily.
cetirizine.warnings: May cause drowsiness, avoid driving. Avoid alcohol.

omeprazole.dosage: Adults: 20mg once daily before meals. Course typically 4 to 8 weeks.
omeprazole.warnings: Long-term use may affect magnesium and B12 levels. Consult doctor before use.

metformin.dosage: As prescribed. Typically 500mg to 1000mg twice daily with meals.
metformin.warnings: Requires prescription. Monitor kidney function. Do not crush extended-release tablets.

azithromycin.dosage: As prescribed. Commonly 500mg on day 1, then 250mg for 4 days.
azithromycin.warnings: Antibiotic requires prescription. Not for viral infections. Can affect heart rhythm.

aspirin.dosage: Pain or fever: 325mg to 650mg every 4 to 6 hours. Low-dose: 75mg to 100mg once daily as prescribed.
aspirin.warnings: Do NOT give to children under 16. Avoid if stomach ulcer. Inform surgeon about blood-thinning effects.

domperidone.dosage: Adults: 10mg up to 3 times daily before meals. Max 30mg per day.
domperidone.warnings: Do not use long-term. Consult doctor if heart conditions exist.

ondansetron.dosage: Adults: 4mg to 8mg every 8 hours as needed.
ondansetron.warnings: Consult doctor before use. Can affect heart rhythm in high doses.

loperamide.dosage: Adults: 4mg initially then 2mg after each loose stool. Max 16mg per day.
loperamide.warnings: Do not use if blood in stool or high fever. Not for children under 2 years.

betahistine.dosage: Adults: 8mg to 16mg three times daily with food.
betahistine.warnings: Requires prescription. Caution in asthma and stomach ulcers.

dolo 650.dosage: Adults: 1 tablet every 4 to 6 hours. Max 6 tablets per day.
dolo 650.warnings: Do not exceed recommended dose. Avoid alcohol. Caution in liver disease.

ors.dosage: Mix one sachet in 1 litre of clean water. Sip frequently throughout the day.
ors.warnings: Use clean water only. Do not add extra sugar or salt.

pantoprazole.warnings: Long-term use may affect bone density and magnesium levels.

diclofenac.dosage: Adults: 50mg two to three times daily with food. Gel: apply 2 to 4 times daily.
diclofenac.warnings: Take with food. Avoid in kidney/heart disease. Do not use long-term without doctor advice.

ambroxol.dosage: Adults: 30mg three times daily. Syrup or tablet both available.
ambroxol.warnings: Drink plenty of water. Consult doctor if cough persists more than 3 weeks.

levocetrizine.dosage: Adults: 5mg once daily in the evening.
levocetrizine.warnings: May cause drowsiness. Avoid driving. Avoid alcohol.
